message: The checker-of-checkers: it runs each of the engine's hard checks against a deliberately-broken example (a negative fixture committed under .engine/_fixtures/) and confirms the check actually CATCHES it. A required check can be green while its logic does nothing — and a green required check reads as verified at the merge — so this proves each one still bites. A check that fails to catch its own broken example, or that has no example and no recorded reason it cannot have one, makes this go red and names the check. Named bound: a green result here shows each covered check still catches its example input; it does NOT show the check covers every way that thing can go wrong, nor that any other check is correct. This rule joins no suite yet (suites: []): it is built and proven under the engine's own tests in this slice, and is wired into the merge checks in a later slice once it covers the full set of checks — wiring it live against an incomplete set would wrongly fail honest pull requests.
